Database Performance Benchmarks 2024

We tested five popular databases under realistic workloads. Here are the results.

Test Configuration

ParameterValue
CPUAMD EPYC 7763 (64 cores)
RAM256 GB DDR4-3200
StorageNVMe SSD (7 GB/s seq read)
OSUbuntu 22.04 LTS
Dataset100M rows, 50 GB

OLTP Results (transactions/sec)

DatabaseReadWriteMixedP99 Latency
PostgreSQL 1645,00012,00028,0008.2ms
MySQL 8.242,00010,50025,0009.1ms
SQLite 3.4438,0004,20015,00012.5ms
CockroachDB 23.235,0008,00020,00015.3ms
TiDB 7.533,0009,50022,00011.7ms

OLAP Results (query time in seconds)

DatabaseQ1 (scan)Q2 (join)Q3 (aggregate)Q4 (complex)
PostgreSQL 162.14.51.812.3
MySQL 8.23.26.12.418.7
SQLite 3.445.815.23.145.6
CockroachDB 23.21.93.81.59.8
TiDB 7.51.73.21.38.5

Methodology Notes

All tests were run with default configurations and no manual tuning. Results represent the median of five runs with warmup. The workload is based on a modified TPC-C benchmark with realistic data distributions.

Conclusion

PostgreSQL continues to offer the best balance of OLTP performance and ecosystem maturity. For analytics-heavy workloads, TiDB and CockroachDB show impressive results. SQLite remains unbeatable for single-process embedded use cases despite lower absolute throughput.
